items.briefing:
Cybercriminals are abusing ChatGPT's shared chat links to host fake outage pages that trick users into downloading malware. If you receive a link to a ChatGPT page — even from a trusted contact — be cautious, especially if it asks you to download anything or enter your credentials. This technique is concerning because shared ChatGPT links can appear legitimate, making them an effective lure for staff who use AI tools regularly.

There are no major Australian-specific incidents reported today. However, Scamwatch continues to track active scam activity targeting Australians, so remain alert to unsolicited messages.

**Tip:** Never download files or enter passwords through a link sent to you unexpectedly — go directly to the official website instead.

featured.title: Australia's sovereign AI adoption stays governance-led
featured.quote: Australian government agencies lag in sovereign AI adoption due to security and skills concerns.
